sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The white diamond moved to (14, 27) where the orange square with magenta center was. Let me check the current state to see what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("=== Key objects ===")
for n in nodes:
    c = n['color']
    if c in ['W', 'O', 'M', 'G', 'p'] and n['pixels'] <= 25:
        if n['boundary']:
            rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
            cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
            center = f"~({sum(rows)//len(rows)}, {sum(cols)//len(cols)})"
        else:
            center = "?"
        print(f"  {c} id={n['id']} px={n['pixels']} children={n['children']} {center}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- W id=9 at (14, 27) - white diamond moved to the orange square position
- O id=10 at (15, 19) with M child - NEW orange square with magenta center appeared at a different position!
- G id=7 at (12, 24) - dark gray square (was the original orange square position)
- G id=25 at (21, 8) - dark gray square
- G id=38 at (35, 45) - dark gray square
- p id=42 at (41, 49) with M child - purple square with magenta center
- G id=48 at (48, 54) - dark gray square
So the pattern is: clicking the orange square with magenta center moves the white diamond there and spawns a new orange square with magenta center at a new position. The new position (15, 19) is along the path towards the left dark gray square at (21, 8).
Let me continue clicking through the path. The next target should be the dark gray square at (21, 8) or the new orange square at (15, 19).
Actually, looking at the pattern more carefully:
1. Start: white diamond at (6, 17)
2. Click orange square at (12, 24) → white diamond moves there, new orange square spawns at (14, 27)
3. Click dark gray at (9, 49) → white diamond moves there
4. Click orange square at (14, 27) → white diamond moves there, new orange square spawns at (15, 19)
Wait, this doesn't match level 1's pattern exactly. In level 1, I clicked through waypoints in sequence. But here, I clicked the dark gray square before the orange square with magenta center.
Let me think about this differently. The path seems to be:
- White diamond → orange square → dark gray (right) → orange square (new) → dark gray (left) → ... → purple square → dark gray → orange ring
Or maybe the path is:
- White diamond → orange square → dark gray (right) → back to orange square → dark gray (left) → ... 
Actually, I think the pattern is that clicking the orange square with magenta center spawns the next waypoint along the path. Let me continue by clicking the dark gray square at (21, 8).
